name: India
heroImageAlt: A man planning a move from India to the Netherlands, with a world map showing the route on his laptop and a canal city view outside. The desk includes a passport, documents, and a travel case, emphasizing the journey of international relocation.

notes: Long-haul route; many Indian professionals relocate through international tech companies.
Planning documentation and housing early is important due to visa processing timelines and housing demand.

commonRoutes: Highly Skilled Migrant visa — employer-sponsored route used by most Indian professionals
EU Blue Card — alternative for highly qualified workers
Partner / family visa — for spouses or partners of Dutch residents
Study visa — for students relocating for Dutch universities
notes: Many Indian professionals relocate through global technology companies.
Route eligibility depends on sponsor and personal circumstances; confirm with official sources.

countrySpecificNotes: Educational certificates may need verification or apostille depending on visa route.

shipping.intro: Shipping from India typically takes 4–8 weeks by sea freight. Many movers initially stay in temporary accommodation.
notes: Common options: luggage-only relocation, shared container shipping, or full container shipping.
Build buffer for transit and customs; temporary housing often overlaps with shipment arrival.
Plan document and customs requirements for household goods in advance.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from India | Visa, Costs & Relocation Guide
seo.description: A practical relocation guide for Indians moving to the Netherlands including visa routes, relocation costs, documents, and planning tools.

faqOverrides.q: Do Indian citizens need a visa to move to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Yes, for stays longer than 90 days. Indian citizens need a residence permit. Common routes include the Highly Skilled Migrant visa (employer sponsorship), EU Blue Card, partner visa, or study visa.

faqOverrides.q: How long does a Dutch work visa take for Indians?
faqOverrides.a: Processing times vary by route. Highly Skilled Migrant applications are often processed within a few months once the employer is a recognised sponsor and documents are complete. Check IND for current processing times.

faqOverrides.q: How much does relocation from India cost?
faqOverrides.a: Costs vary by household size, relocation packages, and housing. Typical ranges include flights (€450–€900), shipping (€2,500–€6,000), temporary housing (€1,300–€2,800/month), and permit and setup costs. Use the relocation cost estimator for a personalised range.

faqOverrides.q: Can Indian professionals work in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Yes, with a valid residence permit that allows work, such as the Highly Skilled Migrant visa or EU Blue Card. Most Indian professionals relocate through employer sponsorship.

faqOverrides.q: What documents are required for Indian professionals moving to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Typically passport, birth and marriage certificates if relevant, employment contract, academic degree certificates, proof of address, and visa sponsorship documentation. Educational certificates may need verification or apostille.

scenarioOverrides.title: Software engineer relocating from Bangalore
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on Highly Skilled Migrant visa, technology companies, and Amsterdam or Eindhoven tech hubs. Plan document prep, housing search, and first-week registration.

scenarioOverrides.title: IT professional relocating through multinational company
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on corporate relocation packages and employer sponsorship. Coordinate visa, housing, and registration with employer support.

scenarioOverrides.title: Student relocating for Dutch university
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on study visa, student housing, and registration steps. Plan for proof of enrolment, funds, and health insurance.

scenarioOverrides.title: Family relocation from India
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on school planning, housing search, and family documentation. Plan for longer lead time on documents and housing that fits family size.

customNotes: Indian movers often compare international transfers, temporary housing, and shipping options for long-distance relocations.
